edges.description: Residual-but-insufficient enzyme allows visceral sphingomyelin accumulation.

edges.description: Pulmonary storage macrophages produce interstitial lung disease.

Residual Acid Sphingomyelinase Activity from SMPD1 Variants

nodes.description: An atherogenic lipid profile (elevated LDL/triglycerides, low HDL) is characteristic.

evidence.reference_title: Disease manifestations and burden of illness in patients with acid sphingomyelinase deficiency (ASMD).
evidence.snippet: Almost all patients have hepatosplenomegaly and an atherogenic lipid profile

nodes.description: Delayed pubertal development can accompany the growth phenotype in NPD-B.

Olipudase alfa (recombinant human acid sphingomyelinase) is the approved disease-modifying enzyme replacement therapy for the non-CNS manifestations of ASMD, with sustained improvements in organomegaly and lung function in type B.

evidence.reference_title: The Genetic Basis, Lung Involvement, and Therapeutic Options in Niemann-Pick Disease: A Comprehensive Review.
evidence.snippet: Enzyme replacement therapy with Olipudase α is the first and only approved disease-modifying therapy for patients with ASMD.

nodes.description: Enlargement of liver and spleen from sphingomyelin-laden foam cells.

evidence.reference_title: Disease manifestations and burden of illness in patients with acid sphingomyelinase deficiency (ASMD).
evidence.snippet: Almost all patients have hepatosplenomegaly and an atherogenic lipid profile

nodes.description: Progressive interstitial lung disease with impaired diffusion capacity.

evidence.reference_title: Disease manifestations and burden of illness in patients with acid sphingomyelinase deficiency (ASMD).
evidence.snippet: most patients have interstitial lung disease with progressive impairment of pulmonary function

nodes.description: Reduced bone mineral density is reported in chronic visceral ASMD.

Biallelic SMPD1 variants reduce but do not abolish acid sphingomyelinase activity. The residual activity is generally sufficient to spare the CNS, so disease is dominated by visceral sphingomyelin storage rather than neurodegeneration.
nodes.id: Residual Acid Sphingomyelinase Activity from SMPD1 Variants

evidence.reference_title: Types A and B Niemann-Pick disease.
evidence.snippet: All patients with types A and B NPD have mutations in the gene encoding ASM (SMPD1), and thus the disease is more accurately referred to as ASM deficiency (ASMD).

meta.association: Biallelic SMPD1 variants with residual acid sphingomyelinase activity

evidence.reference_title: Disease manifestations and burden of illness in patients with acid sphingomyelinase deficiency (ASMD).
evidence.snippet: Acid sphingomyelinase deficiency (ASMD), a rare lysosomal storage disease, is an autosomal recessive genetic disorder caused by different SMPD1 mutations.

nodes.description: Growth restriction and short stature are common in chronic visceral ASMD.
Cytopenias, especially thrombocytopenia, from hypersplenism and marrow involvement.

evidence.reference_title: Disease manifestations and burden of illness in patients with acid sphingomyelinase deficiency (ASMD).

nodes.description: Sphingomyelin accumulates in macrophages of the spleen, liver, lung, and bone marrow, forming lipid-laden foam cells. The reticuloendothelial and pulmonary storage burden drives the visceral manifestations of NPD-B with relative sparing of the CNS.
nodes.id: Visceral Sphingomyelin Storage in the Reticuloendothelial System

evidence.reference_title: Types A and B Niemann-Pick disease.
evidence.snippet: Type B patients also have hepatosplenomegaly and pathologic alterations of their lungs, but there are usually no CNS signs.
